content_markdown:
The diagram shows the pressure–volume ($PV$) relationship for a gas.

Which of the following area(s) is/are equal to the work done by the gas as it expands?

diagram_metadata.description: A P-V diagram showing a closed cycle. The expansion occurs along the top curve (from left to right). The area under this top curve is divided into two parts: 'area I' is the area enclosed by the cycle, and 'area II' is the area between the bottom curve of the cycle and the volume axis.

vibe_explanation: Work done by a gas during expansion is the area under the specific path of expansion on a P-V diagram. In this cycle, the expansion occurs along the upper curve. The total area under this upper curve down to the V-axis consists of both the enclosed area (area I) and the area below the lower curve (area II).
